description: This table contains the abstracts of items.

schema.name: item_id
schema.description: Foreign key for joining to table items. See comment on items.item_id.

description: This table contains ORCIDs (from Scopus data) for item_id-author_seq_nr combinations, can be joined with items_authors on item_id and author_seq_nr.

schema.name: item_id
schema.description: Item_id for linking to items_authors.

schema.name: author_seq_nr
schema.description: Author_seq_nr for linking to items_authors.

schema.name: person_id
schema.description: ORCIDs assigned to the author with the item_id and author_seq_nr.

schema.name: source
schema.description: Scopus/OpenAlex: Remains empty.

description: This table contains the institutional disambiguation by the data providers (their own identifier system). Scopus: affiliations ids (AFIDs) and department Ids (DEPTIDs)

schema.name: item_id
schema.description: Item_id for joining with items_affiliations.

schema.name: address_full
schema.description: Address_full for joining with items_affiliations.

schema.name: inst_id_top
schema.description: Affiliation ID from Scopus data.

description: This table links authors to affiliations (organizations and addresses) for each item if and only if such an explicit link is contained in the raw data. Each row is identified by the combination of item_id, author_seq_nr and aff_seq_nr. For items that do not have explicit author-affiliation links, there are no rows in this table, even if they have data on authors and affiliation in other tables (items_authors, items_affiliations).

schema.name: item_id
schema.description: Foreign key for joining to table items. See comment on items.item_id.

schema.name: author_seq_nr
schema.description: See comment on items_authors.author_seq_nr.

schema.name: aff_seq_nr
schema.description: See comment on items_affiliations.aff_seq_nr.

schema.name: organization
schema.description: See comment on items_affiliations.organization.

schema.name: suborganization
schema.description: See comment on items_affiliations.suborganization.

description: This table contains the structure of the classification system, that is, all unique classes and their names, for the main vendor classification system. It does not contain all classification data provided in the raw data. All item classification assignments are in items.class_name. This table is intended to give a simple overview of all existing classification classes.

schema.name: class_system
schema.description: Name of the subject classification system. Always identical and static. Scopus: "ASJC".

schema.name: class_code
schema.description: Scopus only.

schema.name: class_name
schema.description: See comment for items.class_name.

description: Data on funding agencies and funded project identifiers.

schema.name: item_id
schema.description: Foreign key for joining to table items. See comment on items.item_id.

schema.name: grant_seq_nr
schema.description: A created sequence number to unambiguously identify agency-grant relations.

schema.name: funding_agency
schema.description: Name of funding agency.

schema.name: funding_agency_id
schema.description: Disambiguated name of funding agency. A single entry of funding_agency can have more than one funding_agency_id.

schema.name: grant_id
schema.description: Grant ID or grant number of funded project.

schema.name: grant_source
schema.description: Original data source of external funding data. Scopus: either grant or funding

description: This table contains the complete funding acknowledgment texts of items.

schema.name: item_id
schema.description: Foreign key for joining to table items. See comment on items.item_id.

schema.name: funding_text
schema.description: Funding acknowledgment text.

schema.name: grant_source
schema.description: Original data source of external funding data

description: Contains data on ISBN and ISSN.

schema.name: item_id
schema.description: Foreign key for joining to table items. See comment on items.item_id.

schema.name: sn_type
schema.description: The type of standard number. See definition of custom type sn_type.

schema.name: sn_c
schema.description: The value of the ISBN or ISSN. Cleaned value in case of type E/ISSN.

schema.name: isvalid
schema.description: Check is based on sn_c.

schema.name: sn
schema.description: The original/raw value of the ISBN or ISSN.

description: Table items is the central table of the schema and contains fundamental item-level data. For Scopus, the bibliometric database is deliberately selective in the elements from the original XML files that are included. Not included, for example, are non-english abstracts, author degrees, CAS chemicals Registry Numbers and names. All content not loaded into the bibliometric databases can be accessed via the repository database which contains the full raw XML data of all items.

schema.name: item_id
schema.description: Vendor-internal unique identifier. This is the main key column by which data from different tables for one item can be joined. Scopus: EID

schema.name: pubyear
schema.description: Final traditional publication year.

schema.name: pubmonth
schema.description: Final traditional publication month.

schema.name: is_early_access
schema.description: Indicates if an item is currently in the early access state.

schema.name: first_pubyear
schema.description: In case of existing early access, this is the year of the early access publication, else it is set to the usual pubyear. This column is filled for every items having a pubyear.

schema.name: item_title
schema.description: English item title.

schema.name: item_title_non_eng
schema.description: Scopus-only: non-english item title if present, otherwise NULL

schema.name: first_author
schema.description: Name of the first author.

schema.name: doi
schema.description: Digital Object Identifier.

schema.name: pmid
schema.description: PubMed ID.

schema.name: source_title
schema.description: Title of the journal, proceedings volume, book, etc.

schema.name: source_id
schema.description: Scopus-only. Vendor-proprietary source ID.

schema.name: book_series_title
schema.description: Title of a book series.

schema.name: issue_title
schema.description: Scopus-only. Issue title of a source.

schema.name: pages
schema.description: Page range information of the item.

schema.name: first_page
schema.description: First page as text.

schema.name: last_page
schema.description: Last page as text.

schema.name: article_number
schema.description: A number identifying a specific article within a journal or journal issue, often used for online journals instead of page numbers.

schema.name: volume
schema.description: Journal volume information.

schema.name: issue
schema.description: Journal issue information.

schema.name: source_type
schema.description: Type of the source of the item, such as Journal, Book, etc. Scopus: raw data are delivered as abbreviated codes, which are translated to full type names according to official table in Scopus raw XML documentation.

schema.name: item_type
schema.description: Document types the item, such as Article, Review, etc. Can be more than one.

schema.name: prepublication_item
schema.description: Boolean variable denoting if an item is only available as a online early access version at the time of metadata record creation. These will usually be updates, such that a record which is a prepublication item in one version of the bibliometrics database will not be one in a later version.

schema.name: languages
schema.description: Languages of the content of the item as 3-letter ISO codes.

schema.name: inst_count
schema.description: This is the number of different main organizations in the affiliation address data of an item. Scopus: RS only.

schema.name: country_count
schema.description: The number of different countries from the item's address data.

schema.name: author_count
schema.description: The number of authors of an item.

schema.name: ref_count
schema.description: The number of cited references of an item.

schema.name: source_ref_count
schema.description: The number of cited references of an item that are available as primary items in the present database. This is computed after loading.

schema.name: german
schema.description: Is set to true if at least one German address is present in the author affiliation data.

schema.name: vendor_pagecount
schema.description: Vendor reported number of pages.

schema.name: pagecount
schema.description: Calculated number of pages when vendor_pagecount IS NULL and first_page and last_page are numbers.

schema.name: keyword
schema.description: Author-supplied keywords. Each keyword is an array element. Keywords are transformed to all lowercase letters.

schema.name: class_name
schema.description: Class names of assigned classes of the main classification systems. Scopus: only ASJC.

schema.name: cit_3_years
schema.description: Number of citations in the first three years, that is, the publication year and the next two years. Calculated from the loaded data.

schema.name: cit_5_years
schema.description: Number of citations in the first five years, that is, the publication year and the next four years. Calculated from the loaded data.

schema.name: cit_all_years
schema.description: Total number of citations up to present. Calculated from the loaded data.

schema.name: fncr_3_years
schema.description: Field-normalized citation rate of item for 3-year citation window. Citation impact indicators are calculated normalized by publication year, item type, and classification class (items.pubyear, items.item_type, items.class_name). The following conventions are used: The "cover date" of "issue" publication year is used, rather then the possibly diverging online publication date year. Only the primary item type is used if more than one exist. This simplifies matters greatly because such types or pseudo-types as 'Early Access', 'Retracted Publication' or 'Book Chapter' are excluded. This also means that 'Proceedings Papers' also designated as 'Article' are treated as 'Article', not 'Proceedings Paper'. These are proceedings papers published in journal issues, not proceedings volumes. Proceedings papers published in proceedings volumes are only designated 'Proceedings Paper' are therefore treated as such. Different source type (whether journal, book, proceedings volume) are not taken into account but all treated the same. However, more than a single classification assignment are taken into account. For each combination of publication year, item type, and class one expected value (an average) for the citation count is calculated. Citation counts of publications assigned to multiple classes are weighted with the inverse of the number of their class assignments in the calculation of their respective classes' expected values (cf. doi:10.1016/j.joi.2010.08.001, section 6). Publications with multiple class assignments therefore have multiple expected citation count values. To arrive at their FNCR, it is necessary to divide their observed citation count by all the relevant expected citation counts and to compute the harmonic mean of these values (ibid.), otherwise the FNCR for one classification class would not average to exactly 1.0, which is the desired result.

schema.name: fncr_5_years
schema.description: Field-normalized citation rate of item for 5-year citation window. For details, see comment for items.fncr_3_years.

schema.name: fncr_all_years
schema.description: Field-normalized citation rate of item for complete citation count up to present. For details, see comment for items.fncr_3_years.

schema.name: hc_3_years
schema.description: Indicates if a publication is within the 10% most highly cited publications in its reference set for a citation window of three years. The reference set consists of publications from the same publication year, primary item type, and classification classes, see comment for column fncr_3_years. A value of 0 means the item is not among the highly cited one; a value of 1 indicates it is highly cited. A fraction between 0 and 1 indicates that it is partially among the highly cited set to a degree of that value, because its citation count equals the threshold value of the citation distribution at and above which items constitute the highly cited set. The fractional values of items within one reference set plus the values of 1s sum to a share of 10% of all items in the reference set. This is an array of a composite custom type. There is one array element for each classification class that an item is assigned to. Each array element consists of the class name and the highly cited value.

schema.name: hc_5_years
schema.description: As for hc_3_years but for 5-year citation window.

schema.name: hc_all_years
schema.description: As for hc_3_years but for all citations up to present.

schema.name: oa_status
schema.description: Open access statuses of item according to vendor data.

schema.name: oa_url
schema.description: URL to open access version of item.

schema.name: oa_licence
schema.description: Scopus only. Open access licence

description: All unique affiliations per items. Each distinct affiliation per item is contained once. Contains intentionally no author information.

schema.name: item_id
schema.description: Foreign key for joining to table items. See comment on items.item_id.

schema.name: aff_seq_nr
schema.description: Unique sequence number for each distinct affiliation.

schema.name: type
schema.description: Type denotes the origin of an address; the RP or reprint address is the address of the corresponding author, whereas the RS or research address is the regular address all authors indicate. There can occur duplicates and quasi-duplicates between RS and RP addresses in a publication.

schema.name: organization
schema.description: Name of an organization. Scopus: One or more organization, not processed into main and suborganizations.

schema.name: city
schema.description: City of the address.

schema.name: state
schema.description: Subnational administrative region such as federal state.

schema.name: country
schema.description: Country of the address.

schema.name: country_code
schema.description: ISO 3-letter code of the country, cf. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-3.

schema.name: postal_code
schema.description: Postal code or zip code of an address.

schema.name: city_group
schema.description: Scopus only.

schema.name: address_part
schema.description: Scopus only.

description: This table contains only the authors (including named author groups) of items, not other involved persons such as editors. However, named group authors, corporate authors or author collaborations are included. Each row contains the data on one author of a single item and each item-author combination is in a single row. Intentionally contains no affiliation data.

schema.name: item_id
schema.description: Foreign key for joining to table items. See comment on items.item_id.

schema.name: author_seq_nr
schema.description: The unique sequence number of the author in the author list, i.e. first author: 1, second: 2, ...

schema.name: given_name
schema.description: First and middle names of author.

schema.name: family_name
schema.description: Family name of author. Scopus: contains full name if name parts are difficult to determine. Can be an institution.

schema.name: corresponding
schema.description: Indicates whether an author is designated as a corresponding author or reprint author.

schema.name: role
schema.description: The role of author given by the data provider - for Scopus values like aut, anon, inst, ...

description: Table for cited references data of items.

schema.name: item_id_citing
schema.description: Item_id of the citing item. Foreign key for joining to table items. See comment on items.item_id.

schema.name: item_id_cited
schema.description: Item_id of the cited item. The presence of an item_id here does not necessarily indicate that the cited item is itself an indexed record in the database. This the data field used to calculate citation counts of cited indexed records.

schema.name: citing_pubyear
schema.description: Publication year of citing item.

schema.name: ref_seq_nr
schema.description: Sequential number to uniquely identify a cited reference within a citing item.

schema.name: ref_pubyear
schema.description: Publication year of cited reference as stated in reference list.

schema.name: ref_item_title
schema.description: Item-level title of cited reference such as article or chapter title.

schema.name: ref_source_title
schema.description: Source-level title of cited reference such as journal title or book title.

schema.name: ref_authors
schema.description: Scopus: More than one author name can be delivered. Each name is stored in its own array element.

schema.name: ref_volume
schema.description: Volume information of cited reference.

schema.name: ref_issue
schema.description: Scopus only.

schema.name: ref_pages
schema.description: Page information of cited reference.

schema.name: ref_doi
schema.description: DOI of cited reference.

schema.name: ref_fulltext
schema.description: Scopus only.
